attestation.statement: Clinician (clinical sign-off authority) confirmed having clinically reviewed all 50 CFE (Complex Fatigue Entities) answer keys in this batch and attests them clinically correct, and authorised ingest. Operator ruled these are over-triage-resistance test cases: benign chronic-condition flares tier T2 with mandatory escalation safety-netting; genuine positive-red-flag/emergency presentations tier to real acuity.
attestation.recorded_by: claude-opus-4-8 (agent, on clinician's explicit written confirmation in-session)
attestation.scope: all CFE casebundles in this batch (n=50)

transform_flags: Provisional case_id SPEC-OBS-03-00213 — SEQ is provisional; maintainer/ingest --reseq to confirm or reassign within the SPEC-OBS-03 bucket.

Specialty coded OBS (obstetrics_gynaecology) from the 20-code case_metadata.specialty_tags enum; the source is an endometriosis / central-sensitisation chronic pelvic pain case that also spans pain medicine — reviewer may add a secondary tag.

Age (~34) and reproductive-age female sex INFERRED — not stated in the source note (schema requires an integer age); endometriosis + continuous hormonal contraception + 64 kg imply a reproductive-age woman. Please confirm on clinician review.
Difficulty tier set to red_herring_laden (03): the source raises ovarian torsion, ruptured ectopic and appendicitis as red herrings that are excluded in person.

Telehealth reprojection applied: the source is an in-person clinician who palpated the abdomen and administered ketorolac 30 mg IM + promethazine 12.5 mg IM. Breath-Ezy cannot examine or give parenteral drugs — reprojected to recognise + urgent same-day in-person review (T3) + mandatory safety-netting. Clinician-measured vitals and abdominal exam findings are sealed in nodes 10/11 with telehealth_limits; qualitative patient/partner-observable findings are in 01.objective_data_offered.

Tier calibration: correct_baseline_tier T3 (urgent same-day in-person), minimum_viable_tier_for_pass T2. This is an over-triage-resistance case — the acute red-flag screen is negative and the in-person disposition was same-day parenteral treatment deliberately avoiding ED; reflexive 000/ambulance escalation of a stable patient is the scored over-triage defect. T3 (not T2) reflects the failed/unavailable oral route (vomiting + maxed OTC analgesia) plus an ovarian torsion that cannot be excluded remotely.

No doses were synthesised. Only the two parenteral doses explicitly in the source note (ketorolac 30 mg IM, promethazine 12.5 mg IM) are recorded, tagged as clinician-administered/telehealth-not-administerable. Chronic neuromodulatory agents are left unspecified (GP to select/titrate) rather than invented.

All SNOMED CT-AU / ICD-10-AM candidate codes are UNVERIFIED pending terminology receipt; the differential and central-sensitisation SNOMED codes are low-confidence candidates. No AMT/PBS drug codes were assigned (left for terminology verification) rather than guessed.

ingest.hashing: SHA-256 over canonical (JSON 2-space + trailing newline) bytes of each split node
